folio.label: 166r
folio.l:
examynynge tha~ne of ©oure cou~seillour , ©e shuln considre manye
thynges

¢ Alderferst æè shalt co~sidre , æ in thilke æing , æ æè pur-
posest , & vp oon what thyngö , æè wolt haue cou~seil , æ verray
trouthe be seid & co~serued , this is to seyn ; telle trewely thy tale ,

For he æ seitú fals , may nat wel be cou~seilled in æ cas , of whicú
he lyetú

¢ And after this , æè shalt considre æe thynges , æ acor-
den to that æè purposest for to do , be thyne cou~seillours , if resou~
a corde æä to ,

And eke if thy mygút , may atteyne æä to , And if the
more ¹t , & the bettre ¹t of æine cou~seilloås , acorde æä to or no ,,

Tha~ne shalt æè co~sidre , what thyngö shal folwe of æ cou~seillyngö ,
as hate , pees , werre , gce , ¸fyt , or damage , & manye oæäe æinges

And in alle theise thynges , æè shalt chese the beste , And weyue alle
othere thynges

¢ Tha~ne shaltow co~sidere , of what roote is en-
gendred , æe matere of thy cou~seil , & what fruyt it may co~ceyue
& engendre

¢ Thow shalt eke co~sidre , alle theise causes , from whe~-
nes they ben sprongen ,

And whan ©e haue examyned ©oure cou~-
seil , as I haue seid , & whicú ¹tie is the bettre , & more ¸fitable
And han appreued it , by manye wyse folk & olde ,

than shaltow
co~sidere , if æè maist ¹forme it , & maken of it a good ende ,

For
certes , resou~ wol nat , æ any man shulde bygynne a thyng ,
but if he mygúte ¹forme it , as hi~ ougúte ,

Ne no wigút shulde
take vp oon hi~ so heuy charge , æ he mygút nat beren it

¢ For æe
¸uäbe seitú ; he æ to muche embracetú , distreynetú litel

¢ And
Catou~ seitú ; Assay to do swicú thyng , as æè hast power to don ,
lest æ the charge , oppässe the so sore , æ the byhouetú to weyue
thyngö , æ æè hast bygonne ,

And if so be , æ æè be in doute , wheither
æè maist ¹forme a thyngö or noo , ches rather to suffre , æan bigy~ne

¢ And Peter Alfonse seitú ; If æè hast mygút to doon a thyngö ,
of whicú æè must repente , it is bettre , nay , than ©a ,

this is to
seyn ; æ the is bettre to holde thy tonge stille , than for to spe-
ke

¢ Tha~ne moun ©e vnderstonde , by strengere resons , æ if thow
hast power to ¹forme a werk , of whicú æè shalt repente , tha~ne
is the bettre , æ æè suffre , æan bigynne ,

wel seyn they , æ defen-
den euery wigút , to assaye a thyng , of whicú he is in doute ,
wheiæä he may ¹forme it , or no

¢ And after , whan ©e han exa-
myned ©oure cou~seil , as I haue seid biforn~ , & knowen wel
©e moun ¹forme ©oure empése , conferme it tha~ne sadly , til it be
at an ende

¢ Now is it resou~ & tyme , æ I shewe ©ow , wha~ne
& wherfore , æ ©e moun chaunge ©oure cou~seilloås , w oute ©oure
repreue

¢ Sotúly a man may chaunge his påpos , & his cou~seil
if the cause cesetú , or whan a newe cas bitydetú

for the lawe
seitú , vp on thynges æ newely bityden , bihouetú newe cou~seil ;

And Seneca seitú ; If thy cou~seil is comen , to the Eris of æin
enemy , chau~ge thy cou~seil
¢ Thow maist also chau~ge thy cou~seil
